title: Cisco Supplier Guide — Security & Risk (MSS, SCV, BCP)

metadata.review_guidance: Suppliers must adhere to Master Security Specification (MSS). Participate in quarterly SCV surveys and BCP self-assessment audits every two years.
metadata.preferred_position: Suppliers must adhere to Cisco Master Security Specification (MSS). Complete quarterly Supply Chain Visibility (SCV) surveys and business continuity self-assessment audits once every two years.

sections.title: Supply Chain Security
sections.text: Suppliers must adhere to Cisco Master Security Specification (MSS), which are security controls communicated to the supply chain ecosystem. Cisco monitors compliance through security audits, supplier business reviews, supplier self-assessments, and security risk reviews prior to business award.

sections.title: Risk and Business Continuity
sections.text: Cisco transmits a quarterly Supply Chain Visibility (SCV) survey to suppliers via the Supplier Collection Form. Self-assessment Business Continuity Plan (BCP) audit surveys are requested once every two years. Suppliers must document recovery strategies and compliance to Cisco BCP standards.
